content:
# elizaos/eliza Daily Update (May 18, 2026)

## OVERVIEW 
Development on May 18 focused on hardening agent orchestration, improving cross-platform stability, and expanding deployment tooling. Key achievements include a major UX redesign for sub-agent narration, the introduction of a CLI for domain-based deployments, and enhanced benchmarking capabilities for SWE-bench.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS

**Agent Orchestration and Capability Detection**
*   Implemented connector capability detection and hardened the orchestrator with new security gates ([#7782](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7782)).
*   Redesigned sub-agent UX to prevent conversation-memory pollution, ensuring cleaner agent interactions ([#7782](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7782)).

**Platform Compatibility and Deployment Tooling**
*   Added graceful Windows fallbacks for `plugin-lifeops` to prevent errors when executing macOS-specific features on non-darwin platforms ([#7788](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7788)).
*   Introduced the `elizaos deploy` CLI command and design documentation to support domain-based app monetization ([#7786](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7786)).
*   Resolved circular symlink issues in Docker containers to fix health check failures ([#7783](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7783)).

**Benchmarking and CI/CD Enhancements**
*   Added a side-by-side comparison harness for SWE-bench Lite to evaluate agent performance ([#7785](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7785)).
*   Enabled experimental arm64 support in the Snap CI matrix and prepared Flatpak manifests for offline-only build environments ([#7787](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7787)).

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
*   None.

## CLOSED ISSUES
*   None.

## NEW ISSUES
*   None.

## ACTIVE ISSUES
*   None.
